title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
subline: Notes on startups, or how to build the future.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- GIANT 0→1 mark dominates the top 2/3. 0 is a hollow ring; 1 is a vertical bar; the arrow between them is the only chromatic accent. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 880)" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-weight="300" fill="#ffffff" text-anchor="middle">
    <!-- The 0 — a 1100px hollow ring, 0a0a0a fill, white 6px stroke so it reads as a frame not a disc -->
    <text x="-340" y="280" font-size="1400" letter-spacing="-0.06em" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linejoin="miter">0</text>
    <!-- The 1 — a heavy 1 with massive serif -->
    <text x="340" y="280" font-size="1400" letter-spacing="-0.06em">1</text>
    <!-- The arrow between them — the only red, signal accent, the book's thesis -->
    <line x1="-30" y1="-180" x2="30" y2="-180" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="14"/>
    <polyline points="0,-220 40,-180 0,-140" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="14" stroke-linejoin="miter" stroke-linecap="square"/>
    <!-- Thin guide rule above the title block to anchor the title to the number -->
    <line x1="-500" y1="380" x2="500" y2="380" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1"/>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: The 0→1 is the book, so the number IS the cover. A 1400px hollow 0 next to a solid 1 sits in the top 2/3. The transition arrow between them is the only red element on the page — the '0→1' idea in literal form. The title 'Zero to One' lives below in a tight 260px mixed-case stack (weight 500) so the type still feels assertive without competing with the number. Subline sits above the title in small ash text. Pill '0→1' is intentionally omitted (we already have the number); brand-mark 'MONOPOLY' in the bottom-right is the only other mark.
